title: Dara's First Day Friends
synopsis: Dara feels scared on her first day at preschool, but when she discovers the classroom pet rabbits need help, she finds the courage to make new friends and feel happy at school.

pages.text: Dara held Mama's hand tight. Today was her first day at preschool. The big yellow building looked scary. Dara's tummy felt funny.

illustration.description: A young South Asian girl with long black hair in two braids, wearing a bright red backpack and purple dress, holding hands with her mother outside a cheerful yellow brick preschool building. Dara looks nervous with wide eyes and a worried expression. Her mother, wearing a blue sari, smiles encouragingly. The building has colorful flowers in window boxes and a rainbow painted on the front door.
illustration.alt: Dara holding her mother's hand outside preschool, looking nervous

pages.text: Inside, children were playing everywhere. They looked happy, but Dara felt sad. She wanted to go home with Mama. A teacher with kind eyes came over.

illustration.description: Interior of a bright preschool classroom with diverse children playing. In the foreground, Dara stands close to her mother looking sad with downturned mouth and teary eyes. A friendly Black teacher with natural hair and a yellow cardigan approaches with a warm smile. Other children of various ethnicities play with blocks and books in the background. The room has colorful alphabet posters and toy shelves.
illustration.alt: Dara feeling sad in the busy classroom while teacher approaches

pages.text: "Would you like to meet our classroom pets?" asked Ms. Chen. She showed Dara two fluffy rabbits. "Oh no!" said Dara. "Their food bowl is empty!"

illustration.description: Ms. Chen, the teacher, crouching next to a large glass habitat containing two adorable rabbits - one white and one brown with floppy ears. Dara points at an empty food bowl with a concerned expression, her sadness turning to worry for the animals. The habitat sits on a low table with child-height viewing. Bright classroom lighting makes the scene warm and inviting with construction paper texture on the walls.
illustration.alt: Dara pointing at empty rabbit food bowl, looking concerned

pages.text: The rabbits looked hungry and sad. Dara felt brave. She wanted to help them! "Can we feed them?" she asked. Ms. Chen smiled big. "What a kind helper you are!"

illustration.description: Close-up of Dara's face showing her transformation from scared to brave and determined. Her eyes are bright and focused, with a small confident smile. In the background, the two rabbits sit looking hopeful. Ms. Chen beams with pride. The scene uses warm orange and yellow tones to show Dara's growing confidence and courage.
illustration.alt: Dara looking brave and determined to help the hungry rabbits

pages.text: Dara carefully poured the rabbit food. Other children came to watch. "I'm Maya," said a girl with curly hair. "I'm excited to help too!" said a boy named Sam.

illustration.description: Dara carefully pouring small pellets from a container into the rabbits' bowl while three diverse children gather around to watch. Maya, a Black girl with beautiful curly hair in a pink shirt, and Sam, a Latino boy with short dark hair wearing a green striped shirt, smile excitedly. The rabbits hop closer to their food, ears perked up with happiness. The scene radiates friendship and cooperation.
illustration.alt: Dara feeding rabbits while Maya and Sam watch and introduce themselves

pages.text: The rabbits munched happily. Dara felt happy too! She had helped the animals and made new friends. School wasn't scary anymore. It was fun!

illustration.description: A joyful scene showing Dara, Maya, and Sam sitting cross-legged in front of the rabbit habitat, all with big smiles and bright, happy expressions. The rabbits are contentedly eating their pellets. Warm sunlight streams through a window, casting a golden glow over the children. Dara's posture is confident and relaxed, showing her transformation from scared to happy. The background shows other children playing peacefully.
illustration.alt: Happy Dara sitting with her new friends watching the content rabbits eat

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Dara feel at the beginning of school?
questions.explanation: Dara felt scared when she first saw the big school building and all the children playing.

questions.question: What problem did Dara find with the classroom pets?
questions.explanation: Dara noticed that the rabbits' food bowl was empty and they looked hungry.

questions.question: How did helping the rabbits make Dara feel?
questions.explanation: When Dara decided to help the hungry rabbits, she felt brave and wanted to take care of them.

questions.question: How did Dara feel about school at the end?
questions.explanation: By the end, Dara felt happy because she had helped the rabbits and made new friends like Maya and Sam.

pages.title: Fill in the Feelings
pages.instructions: Choose the right feeling word for each sentence!

sentences.text: Dara felt _____ on her first day at school.

pages.title: What Happened Next?
pages.instructions: Pick the word that tells what Dara did!

sentences.text: Dara wanted to _____ the hungry rabbits.
At the end, Dara felt _____ at school.
